skill.name: meta-funnel-plot
skill.description: Generate Meta-analysis funnel plots and perform publication bias testing. Takes CSV file with Meta-analysis data as input, outputs funnel plot PNG, Egger test and Begg test results.
skill.summary: Generate funnel plots and test for publication bias in meta-analysis studies using CSV input data.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 43 static findings. All 40 high-severity findings (weak cryptographic algorithm x12, Ruby backtick execution x22, system reconnaissance x2, hardcoded URL x2, network x2) are FALSE POSITIVES caused by scanner misinterpreting Markdown code fences and JSON text content. No actual cryptographic algorithms, shell backtick execution, or malicious URLs exist in this skill. Three true findings remain: one os.makedirs call (filesystem, low risk) and two subprocess.run calls (external_commands, medium risk) that execute R/Python scripts with user-provided paths. These are inherent to the skill's CLI design as a data analysis tool. No evidence of malware, data exfiltration, credential theft, or prompt injection. The skill is a legitimate meta-analysis funnel plot generator for medical research.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - FALSE POSITIVE
high_findings.description: Static scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithms in audit JSON and SKILL.md files. These files contain no cryptographic operations. The audit JSON (line 72) contains a text note about security gaps. SKILL.md lines contain markdown table separators, horizontal rules, and documentation text. Scanner misclassified markdown structural characters as crypto algorithm signatures.

low_findings.title: Subprocess Execution with User-Provided Paths
low_findings.description: subprocess.run calls in run_funnel_plot.py:23 and :55 execute Rscript and Python scripts with command-line arguments from sys.argv. The path arguments come from the user, which is inherent to the CLI tool design. No shell injection vectors beyond normal CLI argument handling. Arguments are passed as a list (not shell=True), preventing shell injection.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operation
low_findings.description: os.makedirs call in funnel_plot.py:191 creates the output directory for saving generated plots and CSV results. Standard filesystem operation for a data analysis tool that produces file outputs.

low_findings.title: Shell Backtick Execution in SKILL.md - FALSE POSITIVE
low_findings.description: Static scanner reported 22 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. All instances are Markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) and inline code (text) used for documentation examples. Markdown backtick syntax was misclassified as Ruby shell execution. No actual shell execution exists in this skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation - FALSE POSITIVE
low_findings.description: Static scanner reported hardcoded URLs at SKILL.md:7. This is a source attribution link pointing to the skill's GitHub repository. Legitimate documentation citation, not a malicious network call.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Text - FALSE POSITIVE
low_findings.description: Static scanner reported system reconnaissance at audit JSON (line 260) and SKILL.md (line 56). Both are plain text notes: one is an evaluation result note in a JSON report, the other is a documentation guideline about output discipline. Neither contains system reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess.run with sys.argv Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: run_funnel_plot.py uses subprocess.run to execute Rscript and Python scripts where command arguments (csv_path, data_type) originate from sys.argv. While the arguments are passed as a list (safe from shell injection), the CSV path and data type are user-controlled. This is standard CLI tool behavior. No code injection or malicious intent found.

content.user_title: Generate Funnel Plots and Test Publication Bias in Meta-Analysis
content.value_statement: Researchers need to detect publication bias in meta-analysis studies but lack a simple tool to generate funnel plots and statistical tests. This skill takes CSV data, creates publication-ready funnel plot images, and runs Egger and Begg tests automatically.
actual_capabilities: Reads CSV files with meta-analysis data for binary, continuous, and survival study types
Generates a funnel plot PNG image with 95% confidence interval boundaries
Performs Egger linear regression test for publication bias
Performs Begg rank correlation test for publication bias
Runs trim-and-fill analysis to estimate the effect of missing studies
Outputs results as CSV files and a formatted text summary
limitations: Does not handle missing or invalid data values beyond basic column validation
Trim-and-fill implementation uses a simplified estimation method
Requires R and Python environments with specific packages installed (meta, metafor)
Cannot generate forest plots or other meta-analysis visualizations

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Publication Bias Check
use_cases.description: A medical researcher has collected outcome data from 15 clinical trials for a systematic review. They use this skill to generate a funnel plot and run Egger and Begg tests to assess whether publication bias may affect their meta-analysis conclusions.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Methodology Validation
use_cases.description: A biostatistician needs to verify the robustness of published meta-analysis results. They input the original study data to generate funnel plots and trim-and-fill analysis, comparing the adjusted effect sizes to the original findings.

use_cases.title: Academic Paper Supplementary Materials
use_cases.description: A PhD student in epidemiology prepares supplementary materials for a journal submission. They use this skill to generate high-resolution funnel plot images and publication bias test results formatted for inclusion in their manuscript appendix.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Funnel Plot from CSV
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a funnel plot from my meta-analysis data in studies.csv. The data type is Binary. Run Egger and Begg tests and show me the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic usage for a user who has a CSV file ready

prompt_templates.title: Full Analysis with Custom Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the meta-funnel-plot skill with data.csv containing continuous outcome data. Save the output files to ./results/funnel_analysis/. Name the outcome BloodPressure. Run Egger test, Begg test, and trim-and-fill analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: Power user specifying output directory and custom outcome label

prompt_templates.title: Validate Published Study Results
prompt_templates.prompt: I have survival meta-analysis data in survival_studies.csv with columns: study, group1_HR, group1_95PercentLower CI, group1_95PercentUpper CI. Generate a funnel plot and check for publication bias using Egger and Begg tests.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with survival (HR) data following a specific column format

prompt_templates.title: Bias Assessment for Grant Proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the file clinical_trials_2024.csv with binary outcome data and run the full publication bias assessment. Generate the funnel plot at 300 DPI, run Egger and Begg significance tests, and explain what the results mean for my grant proposal conclusions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requesting interpretation of statistical results

output_examples.input: A CSV file with 12 studies containing binary outcome data (events and sample sizes for experimental and control groups).
output: A funnel plot PNG image showing study-level effect sizes plotted against precision, with 95% confidence interval funnel lines and a pooled effect estimate line.
An Egger test result CSV with intercept = 0.32, p = 0.18 (no significant publication bias detected).
A Begg test result CSV with Kendall tau = 0.15, p = 0.42 (no significant publication bias detected).

output_examples.input: A CSV file with 8 continuous outcome studies (sample sizes, means, and standard deviations for both groups).
output: A funnel plot PNG with study effect sizes (SMD) on the x-axis and precision on the y-axis.
Egger test showing intercept = 1.24, p = 0.03 (significant publication bias detected).
Trim-and-fill analysis estimating 3 missing studies with adjusted effect size changing from 0.45 to 0.32.

output_examples.input: A CSV file with survival outcome data including hazard ratios and 95 percent confidence intervals for 10 studies.
output: A funnel plot PNG with log(HR) values on the x-axis and precision on the y-axis.
Formatted text output with study count, test statistics, and conclusions written to the console.

best_practices: Validate that your CSV file has the correct column names for the data type before running the analysis. Refer to the SKILL.md data format tables for exact column requirements.
Include at least 10 studies in your dataset for publication bias tests to have meaningful statistical power. Tests with fewer than 3 studies will not run.
Review the generated funnel plot visually for asymmetry in addition to checking p-values. Statistical tests can miss asymmetry in small meta-analyses.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-meta-analysis data or single-study analysis. The funnel plot and bias tests only make sense in the context of multiple studies examining the same research question.
Do not interpret publication bias test results in isolation. A non-significant Egger test does not prove absence of bias, especially with fewer than 10 studies.
Do not provide CSV files with missing columns or incorrect data types. The script will fail with a validation error and you will need to correct the file.

faq.question: What data types does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports three data types: Binary (odds ratios), Continuity (standardized mean differences), and Survival (hazard ratios). Each type requires specific columns in the CSV file.

faq.question: How many studies do I need for publication bias testing?
faq.answer: At least 2 studies are needed to draw a funnel plot. At least 3 studies are required for the Egger and Begg publication bias tests. For meaningful results, 10 or more studies is recommended.

faq.question: What is the difference between Egger and Begg tests?
faq.answer: Egger test uses linear regression to detect funnel plot asymmetry. Begg test uses rank correlation (Kendall tau). Egger test has more statistical power but both are standard in meta-analysis.

faq.question: What output files are generated?
faq.answer: The skill generates a funnel plot PNG image, a funnel data CSV, an Egger test results CSV, a Begg test results CSV, and optionally a trim-and-fill analysis CSV.

faq.question: Can I customize the output directory?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify the output directory as the fourth argument. If no output directory is provided, the CSV file directory is used as the default.

faq.question: What R packages are needed for the R script execution?
faq.answer: The R script requires the meta, metafor, and stringr packages. If these are not installed, the skill will automatically fall back to the Python implementation.
